classifiers.semantic_rule: The universal fallback — use for anything without a dedicated classifier, especially abstract things and people in casual speech.

sentences.en: Give me an apple.
He is a good person.
I have a question I'd like to ask you.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Bound volumes — anything with a spine and pages you can flip through.

sentences.en: This book is very interesting.
I bought two magazines.
There are three dictionaries on the table.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Mouths — family members counted by mouths to feed, or mouthfuls of food/drink/air.

sentences.en: There are four people in my family.
Have a sip of water.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Chunks and lumps — solid pieces you could hold in your hand, plus the colloquial word for yuan.

sentences.en: This piece of cake is delicious.
How much is it in total?
Give me a piece of chocolate.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Cups and glasses — any drinkable liquid measured by the cup or glass.

sentences.en: Please give me a cup of tea.
He drinks three cups of coffee every day.

classifiers.semantic_rule: An indefinite quantity — 'some' of anything. Only pairs with 一 (never 两些, 三些).

sentences.en: I want to buy some fruit.
There are still some unresolved questions.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Small-to-medium animals and single items from a natural pair (one hand, one ear). Not for large livestock.

sentences.en: She keeps two cats.
There is a bird in the tree.
He stretched out one hand.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Flat surfaces you could spread out or lay things on — paper, tables, beds, faces, mouths (flat when open).

sentences.en: Please give me a sheet of paper.
This table is too small.
I bought two movie tickets.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Individual items — upper-body garments (not pants), luggage pieces, matters/affairs, and countable things in general.

sentences.en: I bought a new piece of clothing.
This matter is very important.
He has two pieces of luggage.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Long flexible things you could bend, loop, or drape — rivers, fish, roads, snakes, trousers, ropes, towels.

sentences.en: This road is very long.
He caught a big fish.
I received a message.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Polite/respectful counter for people — teachers, guests, professionals. More formal than 个.

sentences.en: This is Teacher Wang.
Three guests came today.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Matching pairs you wear or use together — shoes, socks, chopsticks, hands, eyes.

sentences.en: I bought a new pair of shoes.
Give me a pair of chopsticks.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Wheeled land vehicles — cars, buses, bicycles, trucks. Not trains (列), planes (架), or boats (艘).

sentences.en: He bought a new car.

sentences.zh: 路上有很多辆公共汽车。
sentences.en: There are many buses on the road.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Things with handles you grip (chair, umbrella, knife, key), or a handful/fistful of loose material.

sentences.en: Please bring a chair.
He brought an umbrella.
There is a key on the table.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Bottled liquids — named by the container. Anything sold or stored in a bottle.

sentences.en: Give me a bottle of water.
He drank two bottles of beer.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Horizontal layers or floors — building stories, layers of meaning, coats of paint, blankets of fog.

sentences.en: I live on the third floor.
This sentence has several layers of meaning.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Any occurrence or instance — the most general counter for 'times' something happens.

sentences.en: This is my first time coming to China.
He took the exam three times.

classifiers.semantic_rule: A complete run-through from start to finish — reading a whole book, watching a full movie, reciting a complete text.

sentences.en: Please say it again (all the way through).
I watched this movie twice through.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Meals and bouts — eating sessions (breakfast, lunch, dinner) or rounds of scolding/beating.

sentences.en: We eat three meals a day.
We had a big meal last night.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Types, kinds, or categories — classifies by species or variety, not individual items.

sentences.en: This kind of method is very good.
What kind of fruit do you like?

classifiers.semantic_rule: Complete sets or suites — a matched collection of items that belong together.

sentences.en: He bought a new apartment.
This is a very good plan.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Events with duration and atmosphere — performances, weather events, games, dreams, disasters.

sentences.en: There was a heavy rain.
I watched a match yesterday.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Portions, copies, or shares — one serving of food, one copy of a document, one share of responsibility.

sentences.en: Please give me a copy of the newspaper.
She found a good job.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Segments or sections — class periods, train carriages, batteries, bamboo sections.

sentences.en: There are four class periods today.
This train has ten carriages.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Written compositions — articles, essays, papers, stories as complete written works.

sentences.zh: 他写了一篇很好的文章。
sentences.en: He wrote a very good article.

sentences.zh: 老师让我们写一篇作文。
sentences.en: The teacher asked us to write a composition.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Stretches or passages — a span of time, a stretch of road, a passage of text, a period of experience.

sentences.en: I've been very busy during this period.
This stretch of road is hard to walk.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Songs and poems — lyrical or poetic compositions meant to be heard or recited.

sentences.en: She sang a song.
This poem is very famous.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Sealed correspondence — letters, emails, telegrams. Originally: things sealed in an envelope.

sentences.en: I received a letter.
She wrote a long email.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Machines and equipment — electronic devices, appliances, and mechanical apparatus that sit on a platform or stand.

sentences.en: I bought a new computer.
There is an air conditioner in the room.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Large immovable structures — mountains, bridges, buildings, cities, temples. Things that sit firmly in place.

sentences.en: This mountain is very tall.

sentences.zh: 那座桥有一百年的历史。
sentences.en: That bridge has a hundred years of history.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Standalone buildings — residential blocks, villas, office towers. More specific than 座 (only buildings, not mountains or bridges).
Rooms — enclosed spaces within a building. Bedrooms, classrooms, offices, kitchens.

sentences.en: I booked a room.
The school has many classrooms.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Businesses and establishments — companies, restaurants, shops, banks. Places run as a 'household' enterprise.

sentences.en: There is a supermarket nearby.
He started a company.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Institutions — schools, hospitals, research institutes. Formal establishments with a public or institutional function.

sentences.en: This is a very good university.
There is a hospital nearby.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Plants rooted in the ground — trees, seedlings, heads of cabbage. Living things growing upright from the earth.

sentences.en: There is a big tree in the yard.
I planted two cabbages.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Flowers and flower-shaped things — blossoms, clouds, mushrooms. Things that billow or bloom outward.

sentences.en: She gave me a rose.
There is a white cloud in the sky.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Small roundish objects — stars, hearts, teeth, candy, pearls, bullets. Bigger than a grain (粒), smaller than a ball.

sentences.en: There are many stars in the sky.
He lost a tooth.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Tiny grains and particles — rice grains, sand grains, seeds, pills. Smaller than 颗.
Liquid drops — water drops, teardrops, drops of blood. The smallest natural unit of liquid.
Long rigid or semi-rigid sticks — chopsticks, bones, needles, hairs, bananas. Straighter and stiffer than 条.

sentences.en: There is a hair on the floor.
Give me a chopstick.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Manufactured stick-shaped objects — pens, candles, cigarettes, guns. Also songs and military units.

sentences.en: Lend me a pen.
A candle was lit on the table.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Natural branches and stems — flower stems, tree branches. Living plant material still on the branch (literary).
Equipment worn as a set — glasses, gloves, headphones. Also facial expressions and couplets.

sentences.en: He wears a pair of glasses.
She bought a pair of gloves.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Matched couples or paired items — romantic pairs, earrings, twins, wings. Emphasizes the relationship between the two.

sentences.en: They are a married couple.
She bought a pair of earrings.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Groups, flocks, herds — any cluster of animals or people gathered together.

sentences.en: A group of children are playing.
A flock of birds flew across the sky.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Large livestock and big animals — cows, pigs, elephants. Animals big enough to be counted 'by the head.'

sentences.en: There are ten cows on the farm.
He raises two pigs.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Horses and bolts of cloth — historically linked because cloth and horses were both units of trade/tax.
Batches and cohorts — goods shipped together, groups of people processed together. Implies a collective lot.
Hung or displayed flat artwork — paintings, maps, scrolls, banners. Things meant to be viewed on a wall.
Major works and complex machines — films, novels, TV series, vehicles, laws. Substantial creations with many parts.

sentences.en: This movie is very good.
I switched to a new phone.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Framed machines — aircraft, pianos, cameras. Things with a structural frame or skeleton.
Watercraft — ships, boats, submarines. Anything that sails or floats on water.
Trains and columns — things that move or stand in a long line/row.
Flat surfaces or sides — mirrors, flags, walls, drums. Things with a prominent flat face.

sentences.en: A mirror hangs on the wall.
There is a red flag on the square.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Doors, windows, and fan-shaped panels — flat things that swing open or fan out.
Walls — specifically freestanding walls or sections of wall that block or enclose.
Things that go on top — hats, tents, mosquito nets. Items that cap or cover from above.
Formal items or tasks — regulations, decisions, projects, awards. Bureaucratic or official individual entries.
Disciplines and skills — courses, languages, crafts. A 'door' to a field of knowledge or expertise.

sentences.en: I chose five courses this semester.
He speaks three languages.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Sustained effort or deliberation — a round of discussion, a bout of hard work. Implies thoroughness and commitment.
Financial sums and written strokes — money amounts, transactions, debts, calligraphy strokes.
News items, rules, or short anecdotes — brief written or spoken pieces with a point.
Bowl-fuls — rice, noodles, soup, porridge. Food served and eaten from a bowl.

sentences.en: I'll have a bowl of noodles.
She drank two bowls of soup.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Plates and games — food served on a plate/platter, or board games played on a flat surface.

sentences.en: Another plate of dumplings, please.
Let's play a game of chess.

classifiers.semantic_rule: Pot-fuls — tea, wine, hot water. Liquids brewed or served from a pot with a spout.
Wisps and strands — thin traces of light, smoke, hair. Delicate and barely visible threads.
Faint traces of color or expression — a hint, streak, or touch of something visual.
Stanzas of ci poetry — the upper or lower half of a classical lyric poem.
Revered objects — statues, cannons. Things commanding respect or awe, counted with an honorific.
Image frames — individual still images in a video or animation sequence.
Weighty affairs — legal cases, business deals, significant events. More formal/serious than 件.
Medical doses — measured amounts of medicine, vaccine, or chemical preparation.
Square/cubic measures and seals — cubic meters of material, official stamps, prescriptions.
Double handfuls — what you can hold in two cupped hands together.
Medicinal ingredients — individual herbs or components in a traditional Chinese medicine prescription.
Legal/financial cases — lawsuits, property transactions, formal proceedings.
Official written documents — legal complaints, government decrees, formal pronouncements.
Banquets and formal speeches — events centered on sitting together (席 = mat/seat).
Doses of traditional herbal medicine — specifically brewed decoctions taken orally.
Compartments and carriages — enclosed sections of vehicles, especially train cars.
Small shops — dialectal/literary counter for storefronts and small businesses.
Pools of clear water — literary classifier for serene, undisturbed bodies of water.
Cupped handfuls — literary variant of 捧, often in poetic or classical contexts.
Openings and holes — cave dwellings, bridge arches, kiln openings.
Completed communications — phone calls, sustained drumming, a burst of cannon fire.
Natural segments — petals, garlic cloves, orange segments. Pieces that separate along natural seams.

examples.english: a lone rider in the dust (poetic)

classifiers.semantic_rule: Mounted riders — classical/poetic counter for horse-and-rider as a single unit.
Wells and springs — water sources that are like 'eyes' in the earth.
Crop cycles and successive batches — harvests, cohorts, rounds that come one after another.

examples.english: there is a gap in the wall

classifiers.semantic_rule: Gaps and notches — breaks, breaches, or openings in a surface.
Small chests and caskets — literary classifier for ornate containers of valuables.
Neat stacks and piles — orderly heaps of bricks, firewood, hay.
Classical letters and notes — formal written correspondence, personal notebooks.
Buddhist alms bowls — round deep bowls used by monks, also metaphor for inherited legacy.
Classical wine cups — goblets used in ancient banquets and ceremonial toasts.

long_things.discrimination_tip: 条 bends (rope, fish, road). 根 is rigid (chopstick, bone). 支 manufactured stick (pen, candle). 枝 natural branch (flower stem).

animals.discrimination_tip: 只 small-medium (cat, bird). 头 large livestock (cow, pig). 匹 horses.

people.discrimination_tip: 个 neutral/casual. 位 polite/formal (teacher, guest). 口 family members or mouthfuls.

pairs.discrimination_tip: 双 matching pair you wear (shoes, chopsticks). 对 couples/matched set. 副 equipment (glasses, gloves).

containers.discrimination_tip: Named by container shape: 杯 cup, 瓶 bottle, 碗 bowl, 壶 pot with spout, 盘 plate.

flat_things.discrimination_tip: 张 general flat (paper, table). 篇 written work. 封 sealed (letter). 幅 hung art.

events.discrimination_tip: 次 any occurrence. 遍 start-to-finish. 顿 meals. 场 events with duration. 番 sustained effort.

buildings.discrimination_tip: 座 large fixed (mountain, bridge). 栋 standalone building. 间 room. 家 business. 所 institution.

small_round.discrimination_tip: 颗 small round (star, tooth). 粒 tiny grain (rice, sand). 滴 liquid drops.

vehicles.discrimination_tip: 辆 wheeled. 架 aircraft/framed machine. 艘 ships. 列 trains.
